trade.summary: Korea-Russia trade has collapsed following Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion and Korea's imposition of sanctions. Korea joined the Western-led sanctions regime and severely restricted technology and industrial exports. Most Korean companies have withdrawn from Russia. Limited residual trade continues in some categories not subject to sanctions. Russia's weapons supply relationship with North Korea has further poisoned the bilateral.
top_products_a_to_b: limited goods outside sanctions (food, consumer items)
disputes: Korea-Russia tensions over Russia arming North Korea (2023-2024)

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Russia relations have effectively broken down. Russia's military support for North Korea — providing conventional weapons and reportedly receiving North Korean ammunition and troops — directly threatens South Korea and has made the Russia relationship an acute security concern. Russia dispatched North Korean troops to fight in Ukraine (confirmed 2024), which Korea views as a direct security threat justifying counter-measures including potential arms provision to Ukraine. Seoul has considered lifting its ban on lethal aid to Ukraine.
recent_incidents: Russia supplies weapons to North Korea violating UN sanctions (ongoing)
North Korean troops fighting for Russia in Ukraine (confirmed 2024)
Korea imposed comprehensive sanctions on Russia (2022)
Korea considering lifting lethal aid ban to Ukraine in response to Russia-DPRK military cooperation

military.summary: Adversarial. Russia's military cooperation with North Korea — including conventional weapons, technology transfer, and deployment of North Korean troops — represents a direct threat to South Korea's security. Korea has moved from cautious neutrality to active consideration of arming Ukraine. Russia-DPRK military axis is a defining security challenge for Seoul. Any Russian military technology transfer to DPRK directly erodes Korea's defense edge.
incidents: Russia confirmed to have received North Korean ammunition (2023)
North Korean troops deployed to Russia (October 2024)
Korea announced intelligence sharing with Ukraine (2024)
Russia warned Korea against arming Ukraine

financial.summary: Korean companies have largely divested from Russia. Hyundai closed its St. Petersburg plant. Samsung and LG suspended Russian operations. Korean banks cut correspondent banking with Russian institutions. Limited residual trade finance exists for non-sanctioned goods.

energy.summary: Korea has minimized Russian energy imports. Previously, Korea imported some coal and LNG from Russia. Active diversification toward US, Australian, and Middle Eastern sources has proceeded rapidly. The Russia-North Korea military axis has eliminated any diplomatic cushion for energy cooperation.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been effectively suspended. Most exchange programs and joint research initiatives have been frozen. Some low-level academic contacts persist but are not institutionally supported.
